safetyRules: resolve or get the document before saving
send the original baseVersion with every save
do not write directly to files when the HTTP API is available

headingPolicy.coreRule: write the section's most important conclusion into the heading before deciding how the body expands it
headingPolicy.routeQuestion: does this document primarily reduce direction error or retrieval cost

tocMustReveal: what can be skipped on first read

headingPolicy.rejectByDefaultSemantics: treat these as low-information labels to upgrade when they are used as the whole heading or as the heading's only real information; they are not banned tokens inside an otherwise specific heading
failureTests: heading only names a category and leaves the conclusion in the body
heading can be dropped unchanged into many unrelated documents
toc alone still cannot reveal the key judgment boundary mechanism or rejected mistake
heading sounds like a document admin or generic manual instead of a writer with an actual conclusion
heading strength is wrong for the document type and damages scanability or retrieval
workflow: decide document type and heading strength before prose
for each level-1 heading answer the section's most important conclusion
only start body drafting after toc carries first-layer meaning

apiPageStructure.tocGoal: Keep the right-side TOC focused on page sections, objects, and methods.
apiPageStructure.itemLevelHeadingRule: keep api/item headings exact and searchable; apply stronger judgment mainly at page or overview level

docsIr.plainMarkdownCompatibility: headings automatically compile into implicit section blocks
authoringRules: do not rewrite old docs just to satisfy IR
define shared blocks only for reusable transcluded content
include targets must exist before they are referenced
rollback retrieval by disabling DOCS_IR_ENABLED rather than changing /docs rendering

resolutionOrder: if title missing, add frontmatter title before linking

relativeAssets.markdownBehavior: relative .md/.mdx links resolve to the matching docs/reference route
relativeAssets.assetBehavior: relative non-Markdown links and image src values resolve to docs asset URLs
preferredLayouts: child directories under the current page folder
blockedPatterns: path traversal outside the active content root
paths ignored by .docsignore unless explicitly allowed
system/private paths such as .git, .shopme, .next, build, dist, and node_modules

mermaidAiImages.v1Scope: unversioned local filesystem documents in docs-cli or development runtimes

providerProtocols.codex-cli: installed Codex CLI owns ChatGPT authentication
providerProtocols.openai-images: API credential is referenced through apiKeyEnv

cacheFreshness.comparison: current cache-key prefix must match the shopme-image filename
cacheFreshness.staleBehavior: keep the old image visible with an outdated warning

mermaidAiImages.hostedBehavior: display persisted assets and the disabled explanatory style menu only
readOnlyContexts: hosted pages without durable asset storage
safetyRules: author and validate the Mermaid fence before generating the finished diagram image
check image generation capability before making a paid request
send the exact zero-based raw Mermaid block index, exact source, and one allowed styleId
never call the image provider directly from a browser or expose its credential
never accept an arbitrary browser-supplied prompt or style fragment
never make a paid image request automatically when rendering or detecting stale state
never write shopme-image or shopme-image-style before the server has persisted the PNG
reread the document after a source mismatch or version conflict
